task_completion.description: Open the target syllabus document in a word processor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A word processor application (e.g., Calligra Words) is open displaying the file 'cs3301_syllabus.odt'.

task_completion.description: Format the course title as a proper title block.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'CS 3301: Data Structures and Algorithms' at the top of the document is formatted distinctly as a title (e.g., larger font size, centered, or bolded) compared to the rest of the text.

task_completion.description: Apply Heading 1 styles to the main sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Main section headers (e.g., 'Course Description', 'Learning Outcomes', 'Course Schedule', 'Grading Policy') are visually formatted as primary headings (larger, bold, or using a specific H1 style from the styles pane).

task_completion.description: Apply Heading 2 styles to the subsections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Subsections (e.g., 'Academic Integrity', 'Attendance', 'Late Work') are visually formatted as secondary headings, distinct from both H1 sections and normal body text.

task_completion.description: Convert inline text into structured tables for course information, weekly schedule, and grading breakdown.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible grid-like table structures (rows and columns) have been created for the Course Information, Weekly Schedule, and/or Grading Breakdown sections, replacing the original plain inline text.

task_completion.description: Apply specified formatting to the body text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The regular paragraph text (e.g., 'This course provides a comprehensive introduction...') shows signs of consistent formatting adjustments (e.g., specific font, alignment, or line spacing applied).

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user clicks the 'Save' icon, uses the File > Save menu, or the save icon appears greyed out/inactive, indicating the file has been successfully saved to /home/ga/Documents/cs3301_syllabus.odt.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical word processor to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a GUI word processor (like Calligra Words or LibreOffice Writer) rather than a terminal text editor (like nano/vim) or a script.

integrity.description: The document contains the original expected syllabus content.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the document includes the expected keywords (e.g., 'Cormen', 'Dijkstra', 'Scanlon Hall', 'NP-completeness') and was not replaced with generic placeholder text.

integrity.description: The agent did not use command-line conversion tools to bypass GUI formatting.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to run tools like 'pandoc' or python scripts to automatically format the ODT file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Document path: /home/ga/Documents/cs3301_syllabus.odt. Expected Title: 'CS 3301: Data Structures and Algorithms'. Expected H1 Sections: Course Description, Learning Outcomes, Course Schedule, Grading Policy, Course Policies, University Resources, Important Dates. Expected H2 Subsections: Academic Integrity, Attendance, Late Work, Accessibility, Tutoring, Mental Health. The document must contain three structured tables (course information, weekly schedule, grading breakdown). Keywords to verify content authenticity: Cormen, dynamic programming, NP-completeness, binary search trees, Dijkstra, academic dishonesty, Scanlon Hall, Ely Hall, Skiena.
